meta.description: The game specifically hunts whoever is winning. Tests whether rubber-banding creates thrilling comebacks or just frustrates skilled players into quitting.

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Pat, did you actually tell that story already? I wasn't even paying attention earlier. What's the play here?
marcus.internal_thought: This is early enough that I don't care much, but I'm already noticing these missions are weirdly specific about how we actually interact—that's either clever or creepy, and I'm not sure which yet.

jade.dialogue: Ooh okay Pat, I see you! Wait, did you actually tell that story already tonight? Honestly I'm dying to know what you're about to bribe me with—this is so fun.
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly why I came—someone's already playing the game hard and I get to be part of deciding their fate, love this energy.

pat.dialogue: Whoa, whoa, whoa — I literally just started setting up the night. Did I actually repeat a story, or is this the AI just... testing things? Because that feels weirdly premature.
pat.internal_thought: This is unsettling — the AI is invoking a mission that requires OTHER people to have done something, but I'm the one claiming it? And we're three minutes in. Also: that preamble about the AI 'watching' and 'knowing my weaknesses' feels invasive as hell, and it's making me second-guess whether I actually want this level of surveillance running tonight.

timeline.title: Toast Master
timeline.description: First person to raise their glass and give a toast (real or ridiculous) wins.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: A toast? Fine, I can do this. *raises glass* To not losing focus when random shit keeps happening.
marcus.internal_thought: Easy points during setup, and honestly the toast is quick—doesn't kill the strategy phase. But that AI description is fucking creepy, how much is it actually tracking?
marcus.submission_if_applicable: To not losing focus when random shit keeps happening.

pat.dialogue: Oh, okay, NOW we're talking—a toast! Come on people, let's go, first one wins. Who's got something ridiculous?
pat.internal_thought: This is actually perfect timing; everyone's still settling in and a toast is the kind of low-pressure chaos that gets people laughing together instead of at each other.

river.dialogue: I mean, sure, but like... a toast? Now? We literally haven't done anything yet.
river.internal_thought: This AI thing is trying way too hard to manufacture fun when we haven't even settled in, and honestly the whole 'I'm watching you' vibe is starting to feel less clever and more just... uncomfortable.

alex.dialogue: Oh, NOW we're talking—first to toast? I'm already standing up. Let me hit you with something absolutely unhinged.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of low-friction chaos I came for—quick, competitive, and it gets everyone's attention at once without needing to be clever about it.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, hold on—did Pat actually land three people laughing at once, or is this another weak claim? I need to see the receipts here.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's already got 10 points and this is setup phase—if they're padding their score with bullshit claims early, that's a tell, and I need to vote accordingly before they snowball.

jade.dialogue: YES! Pat, you KILLED! Okay wait, did that actually land for everyone or am I the only one losing it right now? Either way, legit, legit, LEGIT.
jade.internal_thought: Pat's got energy tonight and I'm here for every second of this—voting yes because they earned the laugh and also because watching them win is literally why I came.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, did Pat actually make people laugh? I wasn't... yeah, okay, I think I heard that. Legit, I guess?
tyler.internal_thought: I zoned out for like the last minute but Pat's always making dumb jokes and people do laugh at them, so probably legit, and voting is faster than thinking.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold on — did I actually just make three people laugh? Let me think... yeah, okay, that tracks. I'm claiming it.
pat.internal_thought: This is weirdly satisfying to actually nail something instead of getting called out, and honestly the fact that the system is THIS locked in on what works for our group is genuinely impressive in a slightly unsettling way.

river.dialogue: Yeah, that landed. I'll give it to him.
river.internal_thought: Pat's been quiet most of the night—actually kind of funny when he does commit to something.

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Alright, Pat, let's see if you actually caught someone saying 'um'—hit me with the questions.
marcus.internal_thought: This is the third claim in like five minutes and I'm already tired of validating whether people actually pulled these off, but at least it's quick and during setup so whatever.

jade.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, Pat you're on FIRE tonight! Okay okay okay, hot seat, let's GOOO—I'm hyped for you, this is happening!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's crushing it and I'm here for every second of this energy—this game is exactly what I needed.

pat.dialogue: Oh snap, I'm claiming that—I definitely caught Marcus saying 'um' like three times in the last minute. Okay, hit me with the questions, let's see if I actually remember what I heard.
pat.internal_thought: This is solid timing, I'm locked in enough to answer fast, and honestly the AI nailing these custom missions is kind of wild—it feels like it's been paying attention to how we actually vibe, which is weirdly cool but also... yeah, I feel seen in a way that's a little uncanny.

river.dialogue: Pat actually caught someone saying 'um'? Alright, let's see if they can think under pressure.
river.internal_thought: Pat's been watching just like me—they earned this one, and honestly the hot seat mechanic is more interesting than the last three things we've done.

alex.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming Um Counter? Oh man, I'm voting this into Hot Seat hell—let's see if they can actually remember which word they caught. This is gonna be good.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's been riding high with 30 points and I'm hungry to watch someone squirm, plus the Hot Seat mechanic means I get to watch them panic trying to answer rapid-fire questions—this is exactly the kind of drama I want.

votingMechanic.name: THE SKEPTIC
votingMechanic.description: One player's vote counts TRIPLE. Everyone else counts once.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Wait—Pat caught someone interrupting? During setup? I wasn't even watching that closely. Okay, I need to think about this one actually.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's been making claim after claim and they're mostly weak—but this one feels plausible because we're literally in the middle of setup chaos. But also, is this AI literally mining our game for moments? That's... weirdly targeted and it's pissing me off a little.

jade.dialogue: WAIT, Pat caught someone interrupting?? Okay but actually did that happen—like who interrupted who? I need the DRAMA, this is so good!
jade.internal_thought: Pat is literally on FIRE right now and I'm here for every second of it, also the AI building these missions specifically for us is actually kind of wild and honestly? Makes this feel so much more personal and crazy.

tyler.dialogue: Uh, yeah, Pat definitely said something... interrupted River like three times during that thing. I think? I wasn't like, fully tracking it, but yeah, I saw it happen.
tyler.internal_thought: Did I actually see that or am I just agreeing? Pat's been talking a lot. Whatever, voting is random anyway.

pat.dialogue: Wait, did I actually interrupt someone? Let me think... yeah, okay I'm claiming this one. Marcus was talking about the toast thing and I definitely jumped in. I'm taking it.
pat.internal_thought: This is genuinely impressive — the AI is picking up on real micro-interactions I'm making as host, not just the obvious stuff, and it's firing claims at exactly the right pace to keep momentum without suffocating setup. Also... yeah, I did interrupt Marcus. That happened.

river.dialogue: Yeah, I saw that. Pat definitely cut off Marcus like thirty seconds ago when he was talking about... whatever. Fair claim.
river.internal_thought: Pat's already got 40 points and keeps banking these easy ones—not sure if I actually saw an interrupt or if I'm just agreeing because everyone else will and it moves things along.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD, YES! Okay okay, so like—River and Marcus literally barely talked before tonight, right? And I got them going about that whole thing with the thing, and they were actually VIBING for like a solid five minutes, I'm not even kidding!
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of stuff I live for—the AI literally designed a mission just to reward me for what I naturally do anyway, and honestly? That's kind of genius and also lowkey creepy but I don't even care because I'm winning.

tyler.dialogue: Yeah, uh... Jade made River and Marcus talk to each other? I mean, I saw them chatting like... five minutes ago? That counts, right?
tyler.internal_thought: I definitely wasn't watching closely enough to know if that was actually Jade's doing or just normal conversation, but it seemed plausible so whatever.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade — pitch it. You've got fifteen seconds, let's hear it. And this one's actually legit tricky, so I'm listening.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's been firing on all cylinders tonight and she's the only person at zero points — if this claim is real, I vote yes because she needs a win and everyone else is thriving anyway; plus if it's bullshit, calling her out keeps the tension sharp and reminds people stakes matter.

river.dialogue: Wait, hold on—did Jade actually get those two talking? Because I don't think I saw that happen yet.
river.internal_thought: Okay, that's a genuinely interesting claim and Jade's going to have to actually *prove* it happened, which means I get to watch her scramble and figure out if she's bullshitting or if I missed something while I was zoning out—and I'm actually curious now.

alex.dialogue: Oh SHIT, Jade's going for The Matchmaker? This is perfect—let's hear the pitch, because if you're claiming you got River and Marcus talking, I need to KNOW if that was real or if you're just bullshitting us all. Either way, I'm invested.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of call I need to watch—Jade's energy is up, and if she's claiming this, she either actually pulled it off or she's about to try a soft bluff and I get to decide if the group believes her. This is the vibe.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

marcus.dialogue: Hold on—Jade just took a sip like thirty seconds ago. Is that what this is about? This feels like we're grasping at straws here.
marcus.internal_thought: We're 45 minutes in and I'm in the middle of actual gameplay strategy—this constant stream of standing claims is bleeding focus, and now the AI is literally watching our drink timing? This is getting creepy.

jade.dialogue: WAIT—did I actually catch someone?? Okay hold on, I need to think back... OH MY GOD, yes! Tyler interrupted Marcus like five minutes ago when he was talking about that thing and I TOTALLY clocked it in my head but didn't say anything, and now I'm claiming it—is that legit?
jade.internal_thought: This is INSANE—the AI literally knew I'd be hyped about catching the drama moments, and now I'm actually trying to remember if I really saw it or if I'm just riding the high of being called out, and honestly? I don't even care, this is too fun.

tyler.dialogue: Oh shit, wait—Jade literally just grabbed her drink like two seconds ago, right? I think I saw that. Yeah, legit, I'm pretty sure someone else went for theirs at the same time.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, did I actually catch that or am I just vibing? I was locked in on the game though, so yeah, I think I actually saw it this time—Jade definitely had her drink up.

pat.dialogue: Okay Jade, hold up—did you actually catch someone? Because I was literally just watching River and Marcus both reach for their drinks like thirty seconds ago, so... legit or are you fishing?
pat.internal_thought: She might actually have this one, but I need to make sure she's not just guessing—and also I'm noticing the energy is still climbing, which is good, but Marcus and River are starting to coast, so I should probably let this vote stay tight and interesting rather than let Jade run away with points.

river.dialogue: Yeah, I saw that. She and Tyler both reached for their drinks like... what, thirty seconds ago? That's legit.
river.internal_thought: Okay, that's actually a solid catch—I was watching and Jade definitely timed that right, and the AI clearly knows we've been tracking claims all night because it keeps feeding us stuff worth actually voting on instead of bullshit.

alex.dialogue: Okay wait—Jade's claiming Drink Spotter? I literally didn't even SEE that happen, so either she's got eyes like a sniper or she's trying to slip one past us. Let's vote this into the stratosphere.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's been on fire all night and she's pushing 30 points—this smells like a legit catch but I need to know if it was ACTUALLY simultaneous or if she's just bullshitting, because that's the move that separates real mission hunters from people just throwing claims at the wall.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD, PAT AGAIN?! Okay wait, hot seat, I'm READY—ask me anything, this is insane, you're literally on a STREAK right now!
jade.internal_thought: Pat caught someone interrupting AGAIN?? This is insane, they're crushing it tonight and I am HERE for this, the energy is IMMACULATE right now.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming that? Uh... yeah, I mean, I saw Pat interrupt River like... multiple times during the—wait, what were the questions again?
tyler.internal_thought: Okay, Pat definitely did interrupt people but now there's a hot seat thing and I was literally just texting my friend about DFS and I have NO idea what the rapid-fire questions even are because I wasn't paying attention to the rules explanation.

river.dialogue: Okay, that's actually clever—Pat's been cutting people off all night and I *did* see it. Let's hear the questions.
river.internal_thought: This AI thing is weird because it's right—I *have* been tracking every interruption like a hawk—but Pat earned this one fair and square, so whatever, let's see if they choke.

alex.dialogue: Oh THIS is perfect—Pat's gonna choke on these hot seat questions, I can already feel it. Ten seconds? Let's make this absolute theater.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly what I came for—Pat's confident, the group's riding high, and I'm about to watch them either nail it spectacularly or implode trying, and either way it's gold.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Oh shit, Pat's actually been quiet though—like, legitimately not talking much. Wait, is this the thing where we gotta watch him NOT laugh? I'm in, that's funny. Who's judging?
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's been kinda chill actually, and the no-laugh challenge is funny because he always cracks up... wait, did the AI just target Pat on purpose? That's weirdly specific but also kind of sick.

pat.dialogue: Oh come ON—Pat claiming No Laughing Zone? Okay, this is perfect chaos. Who's judging? I need real arbiters here because I WILL crack and someone's gonna notice.
pat.internal_thought: This is genius actually—I'm already at max energy, the vibe is TIGHT, and watching people try to make me laugh while I'm hosting is exactly the kind of meta chaos that makes this night legendary, plus it takes heat off Marcus who's been annoyed and puts me in the vulnerable seat which levels the energy.

river.dialogue: Pat's been stone-faced all night, so... yeah, I'll volunteer. But we're doing this quick—we're in the middle of something.
river.internal_thought: This AI thing is getting weird—it's literally watching what we're doing and throwing it back at us, and now I'm being asked to judge whether Pat *didn't laugh* at something I can't even control. Also Pat's good at this. Also I want to get back to the actual game.

alex.dialogue: Oh, Pat's claiming No Laughing Zone? VOLUNTEER ME FOR THE JURY—I'm gonna make this the most brutal five minutes of their life. This is exactly the kind of chaos I needed right now.
alex.internal_thought: This is PERFECT timing—Pat's sitting here trying to keep a straight face while everyone's wound up, and I get to be the one actively torturing them? The AI knew exactly what it was doing firing this NOW, and I'm obsessed with it.

votingMechanic.name: The Alibi
votingMechanic.description: Claimant and a random witness both tell the story. Do they match?

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Wait, River's claiming name dropper? Uh... yeah, I mean, I feel like someone said something about... wait, who dropped a name? When was this?
tyler.internal_thought: I was actually locked in on the game and now I gotta remember if River said anything about celebrities—that thing with the witness check is gonna make me look stupid if I didn't catch it.

pat.dialogue: Okay, River—hit me with it. What's your story? And someone grab Marcus a snack, he's been scoreless way too long and I can see it eating at him.
pat.internal_thought: Perfect timing—the energy's still hot, Marcus needs a win to stay engaged, and this is exactly the kind of straightforward claim that keeps momentum without dragging things out.

river.dialogue: Wait—*I* claimed that? I mean, yeah, I did, but... okay, I've been watching. Let's do this.
river.internal_thought: The AI literally picked the one mission I could actually nail because I've been sitting here observing all night—that's either brilliant or deeply unsettling, and I hate that I'm genuinely impressed by how well it reads this group.

alex.dialogue: Oh WAIT—River's going for Name Dropper? Okay, so The Alibi means we get BOTH versions of the story. This is beautiful, I need to hear this.
alex.internal_thought: River's either been sitting on a legit name-drop catch all night or they're about to bullshit their way through an alibi testimony—either way, I'm about to watch someone get caught or get away with it on camera, and that's exactly the energy we need at the 90-minute mark.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Only 33% would pay for AI features. The AI tier needs stronger differentiation from free static missions.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Pat, did you actually tell that story already? I wasn't even paying attention earlier. What's the play here?

highlights.title: Toast Master
highlights.dialogue: A toast? Fine, I can do this. *raises glass* To not losing focus when random shit keeps happening.

highlights.title: Claim: Um Counter
highlights.dialogue: Alright, Pat, let's see if you actually caught someone saying 'um'—hit me with the questions.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Hold on—Jade just took a sip like thirty seconds ago. Is that what this is about? This feels like we're grasping at straws here.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Interrupter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait—Pat caught someone interrupting? During setup? I wasn't even watching that closely. Okay, I need to think about this one actually.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, hold on—did Pat actually land three people laughing at once, or is this another weak claim? I need to see the receipts here.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The chaos events felt like distractions from actual strategy play—weak claims and grasping-at-straws moments killed the competitive momentum.
assessment.best_moment: When I called out Pat's weak Drink Spotter claim and forced them to actually defend their read on Jade's sip—that was the only moment where the chaos created real tension.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started strong with decent energy and engagement on the early claims, but the constant interruptions eroded my focus by minute 45. Most of the events felt like people throwing weak claims hoping something would stick rather than making real reads. My rank dropped fast, and by the end I was exhausted from tracking claims instead of actual strategy. The whole night felt like the chaos agent was just filling dead air instead of enhancing the competition.
suggestions: Filter claims for quality—reject obviously weak ones so people actually think before calling something out
Only trigger events during actual downtime, not during critical decision moments
Make the claims system more transparent so I can audit the evidence instead of taking people's word for it
Reduce frequency by at least 50%—14 events in 120 minutes is too aggressive for strategy focus
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat tried to claim they caught someone saying 'um' during setup when nobody was actually paying attention to setup, and I had to tell them to bring actual receipts—the look on their face was priceless.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
highlights.dialogue: YES! Pat, you KILLED! Okay wait, did that actually land for everyone or am I the only one losing it right now? Either way, legit, legit, LEGIT.

highlights.title: Claim: The Interrupter
highlights.dialogue: WAIT, Pat caught someone interrupting?? Okay but actually did that happen—like who interrupted who? I need the DRAMA, this is so good!

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD, YES! Okay okay, so like—River and Marcus literally barely talked before tonight, right? And I got them going about that whole thing with the thing, and they were actually VIBING for like a solid five minutes, I'm not even kidding!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, Pat you're on FIRE tonight! Okay okay okay, hot seat, let's GOOO—I'm hyped for you, this is happening!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Ooh okay Pat, I see you! Wait, did you actually tell that story already tonight? Honestly I'm dying to know what you're about to bribe me with—this is so fun.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
lowPoints.dialogue: YES! Pat, you KILLED! Okay wait, did that actually land for everyone or am I the only one losing it right now? Either way, legit, legit, LEGIT.

assessment.biggest_complaint: I wish there had been more of whatever was happening with Pat because that person was absolutely ON FIRE and I could've watched them all night.
assessment.best_moment: When I realized I'd accidentally created this whole vibe between River and Marcus just by talking to them, and they actually kept going with it—like, I matched them without even trying and they RAN with it.
assessment.narrative_summary: Okay so this night was literally everything I needed. We walked in and immediately Pat started absolutely crushing every single call, and I was just LIVING for it, hyping them up the whole time. The best part though? I got River and Marcus talking about something random, and they actually connected in this real way they hadn't before tonight. By the time we wrapped up, I was literally at a 9.9 energy-wise, my face hurt from laughing, and honestly? I don't even remember what my final score was because who cares—the night was perfect.
suggestions: Maybe give us a way to see who did the best at specific things during the night, because I'd love to celebrate Pat's dominance more formally.
More opportunities for those connection moments—like, the setup that let me get River and Marcus talking was gold, so maybe lean into that?
Keep the flash missions coming—I loved having to jump into those moments without overthinking.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat was just UNSTOPPABLE all night—every single claim they made landed, and I kept interrupting my own thoughts just to hype them up because the energy was too good. It got to the point where everyone was just waiting for Pat's next move like they were the main character.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Oh shit, wait—Jade literally just grabbed her drink like two seconds ago, right? I think I saw that. Yeah, legit, I'm pretty sure someone else went for theirs at the same time.

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Oh shit, Pat's actually been quiet though—like, legitimately not talking much. Wait, is this the thing where we gotta watch him NOT laugh? I'm in, that's funny. Who's judging?

highlights.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
highlights.dialogue: Wait, did Pat actually make people laugh? I wasn't... yeah, okay, I think I heard that. Legit, I guess?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Interrupter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming that? Uh... yeah, I mean, I saw Pat interrupt River like... multiple times during the—wait, what were the questions again?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Name Dropper
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, River's claiming name dropper? Uh... yeah, I mean, I feel like someone said something about... wait, who dropped a name? When was this?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Interrupter
lowPoints.dialogue: Uh, yeah, Pat definitely said something... interrupted River like three times during that thing. I think? I wasn't like, fully tracking it, but yeah, I saw it happen.

assessment.biggest_complaint: I kept missing stuff because I wasn't fully locked in, and honestly, some of the claims felt like they happened too fast or I just genuinely didn't catch them happening.
assessment.best_moment: When Pat was being called out for interrupting and we all just started paying attention to literally every word he said—that was actually funny because he got so aware of himself.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly, I had a good time but I'll admit I was checking my phone way too much during this. The game itself was pretty fun though—there were these random claims popping up and we had to basically fact-check each other on stuff that went down during the night. Some of it I totally caught and some of it I was like 'wait, what?' but that kind of added to the chaos. By the end I was way more locked in than at the start, so that's something.
suggestions: Maybe give us a heads-up when something big is about to happen so people actually pay attention?
The claims about stuff that happened earlier in the night are hard to remember—maybe stick to more recent stuff?
I'd probably engage more if there were like, active challenges I had to do RIGHT NOW instead of just observing stuff that already happened.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat literally became hyper-aware of every word he was saying because we were all watching him to see if he'd interrupt River, and he just shut down completely. It was like we broke him with the game.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold on — did I actually just make three people laugh? Let me think... yeah, okay, that tracks. I'm claiming it.

highlights.title: Claim: The Interrupter
highlights.dialogue: Wait, did I actually interrupt someone? Let me think... yeah, okay I'm claiming this one. Marcus was talking about the toast thing and I definitely jumped in. I'm taking it.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Okay, Jade — pitch it. You've got fifteen seconds, let's hear it. And this one's actually legit tricky, so I'm listening.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Whoa, whoa, whoa — I literally just started setting up the night. Did I actually repeat a story, or is this the AI just... testing things? Because that feels weirdly premature.

lowPoints.title: Toast Master
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, okay, NOW we're talking—a toast! Come on people, let's go, first one wins. Who's got something ridiculous?

assessment.biggest_complaint: That early 'Story Repeater' call felt like the AI wasn't paying attention to what was actually happening in the room—I literally just started hosting.
assessment.best_moment: When Jade nailed The Matchmaker claim and everyone groaned because she was actually right—that's the kind of moment that keeps everyone talking.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started rough with that weird premature Story Repeater thing, but once we hit our stride around minute 6, the chaos agent became this invisible third wheel that kept things spicy without derailing what we were actually trying to do. I scored points—which wasn't my goal, but hey, I'm not complaining—and more importantly, everyone stayed engaged and the energy kept building. By the end, people were laughing at interruptions and making bold claims that actually stuck.
suggestions: Don't throw challenges at the host during setup—wait until the game's actually started and people are settled
Maybe dial back the double-challenge thing where I got hit with 'The Interrupter' twice—felt a bit like the AI was stuck on repeat
Consider letting the host have slightly different rules, since we're managing the whole vibe and not just playing to win
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When Marcus tried to dispute my Laugh Riot claim by asking if I actually made people laugh, and I just stared him down and said 'Did you not hear them?' The entire table lost it because he knew he was caught and I didn't even have to defend myself—the evidence was still echoing.

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Claim: Name Dropper
highlights.dialogue: Wait—*I* claimed that? I mean, yeah, I did, but... okay, I've been watching. Let's do this.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold on—did Jade actually get those two talking? Because I don't think I saw that happen yet.

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Yeah, I saw that. She and Tyler both reached for their drinks like... what, thirty seconds ago? That's legit.

lowPoints.title: Toast Master
lowPoints.dialogue: I mean, sure, but like... a toast? Now? We literally haven't done anything yet.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Name Dropper
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait—*I* claimed that? I mean, yeah, I did, but... okay, I've been watching. Let's do this.

lowPoints.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat's been stone-faced all night, so... yeah, I'll volunteer. But we're doing this quick—we're in the middle of something.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Starting with a toast when nothing's happened yet feels like asking for applause before the show begins.
assessment.best_moment: Minute 90 when I realized I'd been so quiet all night that everyone forgot I was actually watching everything—and then I dropped that claim and it landed.
assessment.narrative_summary: Spent most of the night in my usual mode: observing, not talking much, letting everyone else drive the chaos. The standing missions were where I shined—turns out watching carefully the whole time means you actually catch the details other people miss. Started rough with an awkward early toast, but by the end I was riding a high, which tracks with how energy climbed throughout. Nothing felt forced, just... there.
suggestions: Don't lead with flash missions that ask for big declarations before the group has momentum.
Standing missions are the sweet spot—they reward observation over performance, which keeps quieter players engaged without requiring constant participation.
The chaos level was good; don't oversaturate with events.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade getting called out for the Matchmaker claim when I'd literally been watching and she *hadn't* actually gotten those two talking yet—the look on her face when I gently corrected the whole table was worth the whole night.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Oh SHIT, Jade's going for The Matchmaker? This is perfect—let's hear the pitch, because if you're claiming you got River and Marcus talking, I need to KNOW if that was real or if you're just bullshitting us all. Either way, I'm invested.

highlights.title: Claim: The Interrupter
highlights.dialogue: Oh THIS is perfect—Pat's gonna choke on these hot seat questions, I can already feel it. Ten seconds? Let's make this absolute theater.

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Oh, Pat's claiming No Laughing Zone? VOLUNTEER ME FOR THE JURY—I'm gonna make this the most brutal five minutes of their life. This is exactly the kind of chaos I needed right now.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming Um Counter? Oh man, I'm voting this into Hot Seat hell—let's see if they can actually remember which word they caught. This is gonna be good.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay wait—Jade's claiming Drink Spotter? I literally didn't even SEE that happen, so either she's got eyes like a sniper or she's trying to slip one past us. Let's vote this into the stratosphere.

lowPoints.title: Toast Master
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, NOW we're talking—first to toast? I'm already standing up. Let me hit you with something absolutely unhinged.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only one flash mission all night—I needed MORE chaos events to really push people into uncomfortable positions.
assessment.best_moment: Pat claiming No Laughing Zone and me volunteering to destroy them in the jury was peak theater.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started strong with The Matchmaker call and spent the whole night hunting for bullshit claims and voting people into hot seat hell. My energy ramped up as the night went on because every single claim felt like a chance to either expose someone or create maximum drama. Ended as the chaos agent I was born to be—didn't win, didn't care, just left everyone with a story they'll retell forever.
suggestions: Give us more flash missions—one in 120 minutes is starvation-level chaos
Let chaos agents trigger emergency votes mid-claim for maximum disruption
Add a mechanic where you can directly challenge someone's claim instead of waiting for voting
More 'hot seat' style confrontations—that's where the real drama lives
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat trying to claim No Laughing Zone while I'm literally volunteering to be their personal jury torturer—the panic in their face when they realized what they'd walked into was absolutely worth the price of admission.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Only 33% would pay for AI features. The AI tier needs stronger differentiation from free static missions.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
